findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'mark piazza | manager, pipeline compliance and r&dcolonial pipeline companyo. 67...' (3x).

findings.description: David Miller's signature image in the HTML captures surrounding text content that also appears as regular HTML text, creating reading duplication that interrupts flow
findings.evidence: PDF page shows a clean signature block: 'Sincerely,' followed by handwritten signature, then 'David Miller / State Mining Engineer'. HTML shows 'Sincerely,' and 'for' as text, then a signature image that includes the phone number fragment '(919) 707-9220 if you have an', 'Sincerely,', the handwritten signature, and 'David Miller / State Mining Engi...' — then 'David Miller' and 'State Mining Engineer' appear again as text below the image

findings.description: Mark Piazza's signature image in the HTML includes 'Sincerely,' and 'Mark Piazza' text that also appears as regular HTML text, creating reading duplication
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Sincerely,' then handwritten signature then 'Mark Piazza / Manager, Pipeline Compliance and R&D'. HTML shows 'Sincerely,' as text, then a signature image containing 'Sincerely,' / handwritten signature / 'Mark Piazza', then 'Mark Piazza' and title appear again as regular text below the image

findings.description: www.colpipe.com appears as plain underlined text in the HTML without being wrapped in <a href> tags. In the PDF email signatures, this URL is rendered as a clickable hyperlink (blue text).
findings.evidence: HTML shows '<u>www.colpipe.com</u>' in at least 3 instances of Mark Piazza's email signature. The PDF shows these as active hyperlinks.

findings.description: Alt text 'Social media icons' is generic and appears 3 times in the HTML. It does not describe which social media platforms are represented (LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram).
findings.evidence: HTML: alt="Social media icons" — PDF shows icons for LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram in the email signature blocks.

findings.description: Alt text for David Miller's signature is 'Handwritten signature' which does not identify the signer, unlike the Mark Piazza signature which correctly uses 'Handwritten signature of Mark Piazza'.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt="Handwritten signature" for the signature on page 2 of the PDF, which is David Miller's signature as State Mining Engineer.
